metadata.title: AgentOS — 適応型インテリジェンスを備えた自律AIエージェントの構築
metadata.description: 適応型インテリジェンスと創発的行動を備えた自律AIエージェントを構築。マルチモーダルRAG、認知メモリ、ストリーミングガードレール、音声パイプライン、21のLLMプロバイダー。オープンソースTypeScriptランタイム。

hero.badge: オープンソース · 適応型 · 創発型
hero.title: 適応型および創発型インテリジェンスのためのポータブルAIエージェントランタイム
hero.subtitle: メモリ、ツール、自律性を備えた適応型、創発型AIエージェントを構築。GMIロール、マルチエージェントオーケストレーション、エンタープライズガードレールを備えたTypeScriptランタイム。

multiAgent.title: 創発型マルチエージェント調整
multiAgent.description: 単一の目標から研究者、アナリスト、批評家、実行者のペルソナを生成。共有コンテキスト、予算、承認により、すべてのブランチを同期。
multiAgent.bullet1: 決定論的ルーティングまたは適応型ファンアウト

toolPacks.title: ツールおよびガードレールパック
toolPacks.description: 検索、ビジョン、コード実行、またはカスタムAPIのための組み込み権限と再試行を備えた意見のある拡張機能を提供。
toolPacks.bullet1: マニフェスト駆動ツールレジストリ
toolPacks.bullet2: ガードレールプリセット+コンプライアンスタグ

language.title: 言語と翻訳
language.description: サーフェスごとにロケールとトーンを自動検出。ペルソナは、選択した翻訳プロバイダーで音声、チャット、ドキュメント全体で流暢に保たれます。
language.bullet1: ペルソナ対応トーン適応
language.bullet2: ロケール別フォールバックルーティング

storage.title: ストレージとデプロイ
storage.description: 4段階オートスケーリング対応の7つのベクトルストアバックエンド（SQLite → HNSW → Postgres+pgvector → Qdrant）。ワンコマンドで全バックエンド間の移行。QdrantとPostgresのDocker自動セットアップ。
storage.bullet1: ワンコマンドでベクトルバックエンド移行
storage.bullet2: Qdrant + PostgresのDocker自動セットアップ

workbench.title: ローカルファーストワークベンチ
workbench.description: 永続化されたSQL、タイムラインスクラバー、組み込みペルソナマーケットプレイスを使用して、ブラウザで完全なエージェンシーをプロトタイプ化。
workbench.bullet1: 推論+OpenTelemetryトレースをライブで検査
workbench.bullet2: アーティファクトをクラウドに同期
workbench.bullet3: 完全なエージェンシーをMarkdown/JSONバンドルとしてエクスポート

capabilityDiscovery.title: スマートケイパビリティディスカバリー
capabilityDiscovery.description: ケイパビリティコンテキストを約90%削減するセマンティック階層型ディスカバリーエンジン。ベクトル検索+グラフリランキングが適切なツール、スキル、チャネルを適切なタイミングで発見。
capabilityDiscovery.bullet1: 3層トークンバジェット型コンテキストアセンブリ
capabilityDiscovery.bullet2: グラフベースの関係リランキング

memoryCompression.title: 観測的メモリ圧縮
memoryCompression.description: LLMベースの3-10倍メモリ圧縮と時間的推論。記憶を統合・剪定・強化する自己改善型コンソリデーション。
memoryCompression.bullet1: 時間的推論が因果連鎖を保持
memoryCompression.bullet2: 自己改善型コンソリデーションサイクル

documentIngestion.title: ドキュメント取り込みパイプライン
documentIngestion.description: マルチモーダル抽出対応の10種類のドキュメントローダー。3段階のPDF処理（基本、OCR、ビジョン）。4つのチャンキング戦略（固定、セマンティック、再帰、スライディングウィンドウ）。
documentIngestion.bullet2: セマンティックを含む4つのチャンキング戦略

memoryPortability.title: メモリインポート／エクスポート
memoryPortability.description: Obsidianボールト、ChatGPTエクスポート、Markdown、JSON、CSVを含む10形式でエージェントメモリをインポート・エクスポート。ワンコマンドで全7ベクトルバックエンド間を移行。
memoryPortability.bullet1: Obsidianボールト + ChatGPTインポート
memoryPortability.bullet2: ワンコマンドバックエンド移行

socialProof.title: Trusted deployments (revealing soon)
socialProof.subtitle: We’re onboarding AI agency teams across finance, operations, and support. Logos and case studies unlock with their launches—stay tuned.

gmiSection.title: Generalized Mind Instances
gmiSection.subtitle: Every agent in AgentOS is powered by a GMI — a persistent cognitive core with personality, memory, and autonomous decision-making. Not just a prompt wrapper: a mind.

channels.name: Channels & I/O
channels.description: Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Twitter, and 32 more. Voice via 12 STT + 12 TTS providers. Every channel adapter streams tokens in real-time through a unified protocol.

guardrails.name: Guardrails & Safety
guardrails.description: Every output chunk is evaluated in real time. PII redaction, toxicity detection, hallucination grounding, and code safety scanning — configurable from permissive to fully locked down.

tools.name: Tools & Extensions
tools.description: Web search, browser automation, image generation, shell execution, social posting. 115 extensions and 88 curated skills. Capability discovery finds the right tool semantically.

orchestration.name: Orchestration
orchestration.description: Sequential, parallel, debate, hierarchical, review-loop, and graph strategies. Tree-of-Thought planning. Mission checkpointing with fork and resume semantics.

memory.name: Memory
memory.description: Ebbinghaus decay, reconsolidation, retrieval-induced forgetting, involuntary recall, feeling-of-knowing, temporal gist, schema encoding, source confidence decay. HyDE + GraphRAG retrieval across 7 vector backends.

personality.name: Personality (HEXACO)
personality.description: Honesty-Humility, Emotionality, Extraversion, Agreeableness, Conscientiousness, Openness. Traits modulate memory encoding, communication style, and risk tolerance.

llmCore.name: LLM Core
llmCore.description: 21 LLM providers with automatic fallback chains. Provider-first API design. The thinking engine at the heart of every GMI.

memory.gmi: Cognitive memory with Ebbinghaus decay + 8 mechanisms

safety.gmi: built-in safety guardrails with streaming evaluation

ecosystem.title: The AgentOS Ecosystem
ecosystem.subtitle: Explore the production-ready AgentOS ecosystem powering adaptive, emergent, permanent intelligence—SDKs, agents, RAG utilities, and deployment kits for enterprise AI teams.

skillsIntegration.description: Browse the curated catalog and register extensions programmatically

hero.title: About AgentOS
hero.description: AgentOS is an open‑source TypeScript runtime for building adaptive, emergent AI agent systems. Built by <link>Frame.dev</link>, AgentOS powers intelligent applications with GMI orchestration, persistent memory, and enterprise‑grade guardrails.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.p1: We’re building the most advanced AI agent runtime for developers who want to create intelligent, adaptive systems without compromising on control, safety, or performance.
mission.p2: AgentOS combines cutting-edge AI orchestration with production-ready infrastructure, enabling developers to build everything from simple chatbots to complex multi-agent systems with {span} memory architecture.

team.title: The Team
team.description: AgentOS is developed by the team at <link>Frame.dev</link>, a company focused on building the future of AI‑powered development tools. We're a distributed team of engineers, designers, and AI researchers passionate about making advanced AI accessible to all developers.
team.enterpriseSupportDesc: For production deployments, enterprise licensing, and dedicated support
team.joinUs: Join us in building the future of autonomous agents.

license.title: Open Source Licensing
license.coreDesc: The core AgentOS runtime, GMI orchestration, and infrastructure
license.footer: AgentOS is open source under the Apache 2.0 license. For enterprise support and production deployments, contact us at <link>team@frame.dev</link>.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about AgentOS — from first install to production deployment.

whatIsAgentOS.question: What is AgentOS?
whatIsAgentOS.answer: AgentOS is an open-source TypeScript runtime for building production AI agents. It provides multi-agent orchestration, cognitive memory with 8 neuroscience-backed mechanisms, multimodal RAG with 7 vector store backends, built-in safety guardrails, voice pipelines, 37 channel adapters, and 21 LLM provider integrations. It is designed to be self-hostable and free.

isOpenSource.question: Is AgentOS free and open source?
isOpenSource.answer: Yes. The core runtime is licensed under Apache 2.0. Agent presets, extensions, and guardrails are MIT-licensed. You can use AgentOS commercially, modify it, and redistribute it under these license terms.

llmProviders.question: What LLM providers are supported?
llmProviders.answer: AgentOS supports 21 LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Mistral, Cohere, Ollama, OpenRouter, Together AI, Fireworks AI, Groq, Perplexity, DeepSeek, xAI (Grok), Replicate, Anyscale, AI21 Labs, Aleph Alpha, AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Cloudflare Workers AI, and HuggingFace Inference. Provider fallback chains allow automatic failover.

language.question: What programming language is AgentOS built in?
language.answer: AgentOS is written entirely in TypeScript and runs on Node.js. The npm package is @framers/agentos. It works with any TypeScript or JavaScript project.

comparison.question: How is AgentOS different from LangChain, CrewAI, or AutoGen?
comparison.answer: AgentOS is TypeScript-native (not Python), includes cognitive memory with Ebbinghaus decay curves and HEXACO personality modeling, offers 5 security tiers instead of basic prompt filtering, provides 37 built-in channel adapters (Discord, Telegram, Slack, email, and more), and supports 6 orchestration strategies including graph-based agent DAGs. It also ships with 88 curated skills and a capability discovery engine that reduces token usage by roughly 90%.

whatIsGMI.question: What is a GMI?
whatIsGMI.answer: GMI stands for General Machine Intelligence — it is the cognitive core of each AgentOS agent. A GMI encapsulates the agent's personality traits (via HEXACO), memory systems, tool access, guardrail configuration, and communication style into a single persistent identity. GMIs maintain state across sessions and adapt their behavior based on accumulated experience.

hexaco.question: What is HEXACO personality?
hexaco.answer: HEXACO is a 6-factor personality model from psychology research (Ashton & Lee, 2004). The six dimensions are Honesty-Humility, Emotionality, Extraversion, Agreeableness, Conscientiousness, and Openness to Experience. In AgentOS, HEXACO traits modulate how agents communicate, remember, and make decisions. For example, high Openness increases memory reconsolidation during recall, while high Conscientiousness strengthens retrieval-induced forgetting of irrelevant associations.

cognitiveMemory.question: How does cognitive memory work?
cognitiveMemory.answer: AgentOS cognitive memory implements 8 mechanisms from cognitive science research: reconsolidation, retrieval-induced forgetting, involuntary recall, feeling-of-knowing, temporal gist extraction, schema encoding, source confidence decay, and emotion regulation. Memories decay over time following Ebbinghaus forgetting curves (Ebbinghaus, 1885), with spaced repetition and consolidation to preserve important information. Working memory follows Baddeley's multi-component model (Baddeley, 2000) with a volatile scratchpad for the current turn that consolidates upward into episodic, semantic, and observational memory tiers.

guardrails.question: What guardrails are available?
guardrails.answer: AgentOS provides 5 security tiers (dangerous, permissive, balanced, strict, paranoid) and 6 guardrail extension types: PII redaction (names, emails, phone numbers, SSNs), ML classifiers for prompt injection detection, code safety analysis, grounding verification against source documents, topicality enforcement to keep agents on-task, and content policy filtering for harmful or inappropriate output.

capabilityDiscovery.question: What is the capability discovery system?
capabilityDiscovery.answer: The capability discovery engine replaces static tool and skill dumps with 3-tier semantic search, reducing token usage by roughly 90%. Tier 0 provides category summaries in about 150 tokens (always included). Tier 1 returns the top-5 semantic matches in about 200 tokens. Tier 2 delivers full schemas in about 1,500 tokens, loaded on demand. A meta-tool called discover_capabilities lets agents self-discover available tools at runtime.

queryRouter.question: How does the QueryRouter work?
queryRouter.answer: The QueryRouter classifies incoming queries into 4 tiers: T0 (direct answer from context), T1 (single-tool invocation), T2 (multi-step plan requiring a planning engine), and T3 (multi-agent delegation requiring orchestration). This classification determines the execution strategy and resource allocation for each request.

adaptiveIntelligence.question: What is adaptive intelligence?
adaptiveIntelligence.answer: Adaptive intelligence is how AgentOS agents continuously improve their behavior without retraining. Five mechanisms work together: (1) Meta-reflective prompt adaptation — the PromptBuilder assembles a different system prompt every turn, dynamically incorporating personality traits, mood state, conversation history, retrieved memories, and available tools. (2) Self-evaluating response quality — the self_evaluate tool scores the agent's own output and adjusts parameters like temperature, verbosity, and personality expression in real time. (3) Personality-modulated cognition — HEXACO traits shape how the agent processes information. High openness increases creative associations during memory retrieval; high conscientiousness strengthens retrieval-induced forgetting of irrelevant data. (4) Autonomous memory consolidation — the ConsolidationLoop prunes weak memories, strengthens frequently-accessed ones, and derives new insights from memory clusters, so the agent's knowledge improves over time without explicit training. (5) QueryRouter tiered classification — the system adapts retrieval depth based on query complexity. Simple questions get fast keyword lookup; complex questions trigger full hybrid RAG with deep research.

emergentBehaviors.question: What are emergent behaviors?
emergentBehaviors.answer: Emergent behaviors are capabilities that agents develop at runtime rather than being explicitly programmed. AgentOS supports five forms: (1) Runtime tool forging — agents create new tools on the fly via forge_tool. The EmergentCapabilityEngine uses sandboxed JavaScript execution and LLM-as-judge evaluation to safely create, test, and promote tools. (2) Self-improving personality — agents adapt their HEXACO personality traits within bounded limits via adapt_personality. Mutations persist with Ebbinghaus decay — strong repeated adaptations stick, while weak ones fade naturally. (3) Dynamic skill management — agents enable or disable skills at runtime via manage_skills, adapting their behavioral repertoire to the task at hand. (4) Composable workflow creation — agents compose registered tools into multi-step pipelines via create_workflow, building new capabilities from existing building blocks. (5) Tiered tool promotion — forged tools progress through session, agent, and shared tiers. Tools that prove reliable (5+ successful uses, >0.8 confidence score) auto-promote for cross-agent reuse.

install.question: How do I install AgentOS?
install.answer: Install via npm: npm install @framers/agentos. AgentOS requires Node.js 18 or higher. For the CLI tools, install the companion package: npm install -g @framers/wunderland.

firstAgent.question: How do I create my first agent?

voice.question: How do I add voice capabilities?
voice.answer: AgentOS supports multiple STT (speech-to-text) and TTS (text-to-speech) providers. Configure a voice pipeline by specifying STT and TTS providers in your agent configuration. Supported STT providers include Whisper (OpenAI), Deepgram, and AssemblyAI. TTS providers include ElevenLabs, OpenAI TTS, and Google Cloud TTS. Telephony integration is available for real-time voice interactions.

deploy.question: How do I deploy AgentOS?
deploy.answer: AgentOS is self-hostable and runs anywhere Node.js runs. Deploy on your own infrastructure with Docker, on cloud providers like AWS, GCP, or Azure, or use platforms like Vercel, Railway, or Fly.io. The runtime uses SQLite by default for zero-config setup, with optional migration to Postgres, pgvector, Qdrant, or Neo4j for production scale.

offline.question: Does AgentOS work offline?
offline.answer: Yes. AgentOS works fully offline when paired with Ollama for local LLM inference. Ollama provides access to open-weight models like Llama, Mistral, and Phi locally. The SQLite storage backend and in-memory vector store require no external services. The CLI auto-detects Ollama and configures the agent to use it.

productionReady.question: Is AgentOS production-ready?
productionReady.answer: Yes. AgentOS includes lets you control exactly how much freedom your agents have — from wide open to fully locked down. It blocks prompt injection attacks, redacts personal data, moderates content in real time, requires approval for sensitive tool calls, budget limits, auditable routing, circuit breakers for tool failures, and rate limiting.

compliance.question: What compliance standards does AgentOS address?
compliance.answer: AgentOS is designed with GDPR readiness in mind — PII redaction guardrails can strip personal data before it reaches LLM providers. All data stays on your infrastructure when self-hosted. SOC 2 compliance documentation is planned. The memory system supports data deletion and export for right-to-access requests.

support.question: Is there enterprise support available?
support.answer: Yes. For production deployments, enterprise licensing, dedicated support, and custom integrations, contact the team at team@frame.dev. Enterprise support includes priority issue resolution, architecture consulting, and deployment assistance.

selfHosted.question: Can I use AgentOS with my own infrastructure?
selfHosted.answer: Absolutely. AgentOS is designed to be self-hosted from the ground up. For self-hosted deployments, your data stays on your infrastructure with SQLite, Postgres, pgvector, Qdrant, or Neo4j. If you want a managed vendor-hosted vector database instead, Pinecone is also supported. Docker Compose configurations are provided for the self-hosted backends.

references.title: Academic References
references.subtitle: AgentOS cognitive memory and personality systems are grounded in peer-reviewed research.

ebbinghaus.label: Ebbinghaus, H. (1885)
ebbinghaus.title: Memory: A Contribution to Experimental Psychology.
ebbinghaus.detail: The foundational study establishing the forgetting curve. AgentOS uses Ebbinghaus decay functions for automatic memory consolidation.

baddeley.label: Baddeley, A. D. (2000)
baddeley.title: The episodic buffer: a new component of working memory?
baddeley.detail: Introduced the 4-component model of working memory. AgentOS working memory is modeled on this architecture.

hexaco.label: Ashton, M. C., & Lee, K. (2004)
hexaco.title: Empirical, theoretical, and practical advantages of the HEXACO model of personality structure.
hexaco.detail: The HEXACO model provides the 6-factor personality dimensions used in AgentOS agent identity.

louvain.label: Blondel, V. D., Guillaume, J.-L., Lambiotte, R., & Lefebvre, E. (2008)
louvain.title: Fast unfolding of communities in large networks.
louvain.detail: The Louvain algorithm is used in AgentOS GraphRAG for community detection in knowledge graphs.

hnsw.label: Malkov, Y. A., & Yashunin, D. A. (2018)
hnsw.title: Efficient and robust approximate nearest neighbor search using Hierarchical Navigable Small World graphs.
hnsw.detail: HNSW is one of 7 vector store backends supported by AgentOS for efficient similarity search.

reconsolidation.label: Nader, K., Schafe, G. E., & LeDoux, J. E. (2000)
reconsolidation.title: Fear memories require protein synthesis in the amygdala for reconsolidation after retrieval.
reconsolidation.detail: Basis for the memory reconsolidation mechanism — memories are rewritten each time they are recalled.

rif.label: Anderson, M. C., Bjork, R. A., & Bjork, E. L. (1994)
rif.title: Remembering can cause forgetting: Retrieval dynamics in long-term memory.
rif.detail: Basis for retrieval-induced forgetting — retrieving one memory suppresses related competing memories.

involuntaryRecall.label: Berntsen, D. (2010)
involuntaryRecall.title: The unbidden past: Involuntary autobiographical memories as a basic mode of remembering.
involuntaryRecall.detail: Basis for involuntary recall — contextual cues trigger spontaneous memory activation.

cognitive.title: 認知記憶
cognitive.subtitle: 認知科学研究にインスパイアされた8つの記憶メカニズム。すべてエージェントのHEXACO性格特性によって調整されます。

mechanismDescriptions.reconsolidation: 想起するたびに記憶が書き換えられ、新しいコンテキストで更新されます
mechanismDescriptions.rif: ある記憶を検索すると、関連する競合記憶が抑制されます
mechanismDescriptions.involuntaryRecall: 文脈的手がかりによる自発的な記憶の活性化
mechanismDescriptions.fok: すぐに検索できなくても答えが存在するというメタ認知的感覚
mechanismDescriptions.temporalGist: 具体的な詳細は薄れるが、要旨と意味は時間とともに保存されます
mechanismDescriptions.schemaEncoding: 新しい情報は既存の知識構造に対して符号化されます
mechanismDescriptions.sourceConfidence: 情報の出典に関する確信は、情報自体よりも早く減衰します
mechanismDescriptions.emotionRegulation: 記憶の感情的価は時間とともに調整されます

research.reconsolidation: 検索行為のたびに記憶痕跡が不安定化し、現在のコンテキストで更新されてから再安定化します。開放性が高いほど書き換えの度合いが増幅されます。
research.rif: ターゲット記憶の検索は、側方抑制により関連競合者を積極的に抑制します。勤勉性が高いほどこの抑制が強化されます。
research.involuntaryRecall: 文脈的手がかりにより、意図的な検索なしに関連記憶の自発的活性化が起こり得ます。開放性が高いほど活性化閾値が下がります。
research.fok: 検索成功の可能性に関するメタ認知的判断。外向性が高いほどFOKシグナルが増幅されます。
research.temporalGist: 時間とともに逐語的詳細が減衰し、意味的要旨が抽象化により保存されます。勤勉性が低いほど詳細の損失が加速します。
research.schemaEncoding: 新しい情報は既存の知識スキーマに同化されます。開放性が高いほど柔軟なスキーマ修正が可能になります。
research.sourceConfidence: 情報源の記憶は情報内容自体よりも早く減衰します。誠実性-謙虚性が高いほど不確実な情報源への懐疑が増します。
research.emotionRegulation: 記憶に付随する感情的強度は検索と固定化の際に積極的に調整されます。協調性が高いほど感情の平滑化が増加します。

citations.rif: Anderson, Bjork & Bjork (1994). Journal of Experimental Psychology: Learning, Memory, and Cognition, 20, 1063-1087.
citations.involuntaryRecall: Berntsen (2010). Current Directions in Psychological Science, 19, 138-142.
citations.temporalGist: Reyna & Brainerd (1995). Learning and Individual Differences, 7, 1-75.

cognitive.ebbinghausAnnotation: 復習なしでは、24時間以内に情報の80%が失われます。AgentOSの認知記憶は間隔反復と固定化を適用して曲線を平坦化します。

sqlite.name: SQLite
sqlite.description: 設定不要、ファイルベース。開発と単一エージェントに最適。

hnsw.name: HNSW
hnsw.description: プロセス内近似最近働探索。高速、サーバー不要。

pgvector.name: Postgres+pgvector
pgvector.description: pgvector拡張による本番グレード。数百万ベクトルまでスケール。

qdrant.name: Qdrant
qdrant.description: 専用ベクトルDB。高スループット、マルチテナントに最適。

pinecone.name: Pinecone

inmemory.description: 最速。一時的またはテストワークロード向け。

neo4j.name: Neo4j
neo4j.description: グラフネイティブ。関係認識検索付きGraphRAGに最適。

semantic.name: セマンティック
semantic.description: ベクトル埋め込みでのコサイン類似度検索。ほとんどのケースでデフォルト。

hyde.name: HyDE
hyde.description: 仮想回答を生成して抽象クエリのリコールを向上。

graphrag.name: GraphRAG
graphrag.description: Louvainクラスタリング付きナレッジグラフによるコミュニティ認識検索。
graphrag.citation: From Local to Global: A Graph RAG Approach to Query-Focused Summarization

hybrid.name: ハイブリッド
hybrid.description: キーワード + セマンティック融合。両方の長所。

emergent.title: Emergent Capabilities
emergent.subtitle: Agents that forge their own tools and spawn specialist sub-agents at runtime

forging.description: When an agent encounters a task it cannot handle with existing tools, it writes a new tool definition on the fly. A judge agent evaluates safety and correctness before activation. Approved tools are sandbox-executed and tracked for reliability-based promotion.
details: Composable mode: chains existing tools via pipeline steps — safe by construction
Sandbox mode: agent-written code runs in memory/time-bounded isolation
Three-tier lifecycle: session-scoped, agent-scoped, or promoted to shared registry
Dual-judge promotion panel: safety auditor + correctness reviewer must both approve
Usage tracking with automatic tier promotion when reliability thresholds are met

synthesis.description: Agencies dynamically spawn new agents with specific personas and skills when the existing roster cannot cover a sub-task. The orchestrator identifies the capability gap, synthesises a specialist agent with tailored instructions, and merges its output back into the main workflow. Requires approval when HITL gates are configured.

loop.description: Agents learn from failures and adapt their approach over time. Tool failure tracking triggers circuit breakers to avoid repeated errors. Style adaptation learns user preferences for formality, verbosity, and technicality. Memory consolidation strengthens useful memories and lets irrelevant ones decay via Ebbinghaus curves.

agencySection.title: Parallel Agency
agencySection.subtitle: Coordinate teams of specialized GMIs with 6 built-in execution strategies. Agents share memory, communicate through structured channels, and execute tasks in sequential, parallel, or dependency-based order.

sequential.name: Sequential
sequential.description: Agents run one after another in a linear pipeline. Each agent receives the previous agent's output as context, forming a progressive refinement chain.

parallel.name: Parallel
parallel.description: All agents run concurrently. Their outputs are merged by a synthesis step that combines perspectives into a single coherent response.

debate.name: Debate
debate.description: Agents argue and refine a shared answer over multiple rounds. A judge evaluates arguments and selects the strongest position.

reviewLoop.name: Review Loop
reviewLoop.description: One agent produces output, another reviews and requests revisions. The loop continues until the reviewer approves or max rounds are reached.

hierarchical.name: Hierarchical
hierarchical.description: A coordinator agent dispatches sub-tasks to specialist agents via tool calls. The coordinator decides which agents to invoke and in what order at runtime.

graph.name: Graph (DAG)
graph.description: Agents declare explicit dependencies via dependsOn. The orchestrator topologically sorts agents into tiers and runs each tier concurrently.
